# Anti-Patterns Reference: Draw.io XML Error Diagnosis

Patterns that ALWAYS produce broken diagrams. NEVER use these patterns.

---

## AP-001: Generating Compressed Content

**Pattern:** Producing Base64-encoded strings inside `<diagram>` elements.

```xml
<!-- NEVER DO THIS -->
<diagram id="page-1" name="Page-1">
  dZHBDoMgDIafhmMJ6nS3qYfdnB5ckUlHE4EQ3HR7+lWpOpeYkP6k/b62hSS82Z+9bPQbcDAk
</diagram>
```

**Why It Fails:** LLMs cannot execute DEFLATE compression. Every Base64 string generated by AI is fabricated and produces garbage when decoded.

**Correct Pattern:** ALWAYS output uncompressed `<mxGraphModel>` directly inside `<diagram>`:
```xml
<diagram id="page-1" name="Page-1">
  <mxGraphModel>
    <root>
      <mxCell id="0" />
      <mxCell id="1" parent="0" />
    </root>
  </mxGraphModel>
</diagram>
```

---

## AP-002: Omitting Structural Cells

**Pattern:** Starting content cells without the two required base cells.

```xml
<!-- NEVER DO THIS -->
<root>
  <mxCell id="2" value="Shape" vertex="1" parent="1" ... />
</root>
```

**Why It Fails:** Cell id="2" references parent="1", but no cell with id="1" exists. Draw.io cannot build the cell hierarchy.

**Correct Pattern:** ALWAYS start `<root>` with both structural cells:
```xml
<root>
  <mxCell id="0" />
  <mxCell id="1" parent="0" />
  <mxCell id="2" value="Shape" vertex="1" parent="1" ... />
</root>
```

---

## AP-003: Reusing Cell IDs

**Pattern:** Using the same ID for multiple cells, especially in loops or templates.

```xml
<!-- NEVER DO THIS -->
<mxCell id="node" value="Step 1" vertex="1" parent="1" ... />
<mxCell id="node" value="Step 2" vertex="1" parent="1" ... />
<mxCell id="node" value="Step 3" vertex="1" parent="1" ... />
```

**Why It Fails:** Each duplicate ID overwrites the previous cell. Only the last cell with that ID survives.

**Correct Pattern:** ALWAYS use unique, sequential IDs:
```xml
<mxCell id="2" value="Step 1" vertex="1" parent="1" ... />
<mxCell id="3" value="Step 2" vertex="1" parent="1" ... />
<mxCell id="4" value="Step 3" vertex="1" parent="1" ... />
```

---

## AP-004: Inventing Shape Names

**Pattern:** Using descriptive but nonexistent shape names.

```xml
<!-- NEVER DO THIS -->
style="shape=server;..."
style="shape=database;..."
style="shape=firewall;..."
style="shape=load-balancer;..."
style="shape=cloud-server;..."
style="shape=api-gateway;..."
style="shape=microservice;..."
```

**Why It Fails:** Draw.io ignores unknown shape values and renders a plain rectangle. The visual meaning is lost.

**Correct Pattern:** Use real shape names:
- Built-in: `cylinder3` (database), `cloud` (cloud)
- Stencil: `mxgraph.aws4.ec2` (server), `mxgraph.aws4.api_gateway` (API gateway)
- Fallback: Use a basic `rounded=1` rectangle with a descriptive label

---

## AP-005: Raw HTML in Value Attributes

**Pattern:** Writing unescaped HTML tags directly in the `value` attribute.

```xml
<!-- NEVER DO THIS -->
<mxCell id="2" value="<b>Title</b><br><i>Subtitle</i>" ... />
```

**Why It Fails:** The `<b>` tag is interpreted as an XML element, breaking the XML parser. The file cannot be opened.

**Correct Pattern:** ALWAYS XML-escape HTML in `value`:
```xml
<mxCell id="2" value="&lt;b&gt;Title&lt;/b&gt;&lt;br&gt;&lt;i&gt;Subtitle&lt;/i&gt;" ... />
```

---

## AP-006: Double-Escaping HTML Entities

**Pattern:** Escaping already-escaped content, producing double-encoded entities.

```xml
<!-- NEVER DO THIS -->
<mxCell id="2" value="&amp;lt;b&amp;gt;Title&amp;lt;/b&amp;gt;" ... />
```

**Why It Fails:** Draw.io renders the literal text `&lt;b&gt;Title&lt;/b&gt;` instead of **Title**.

**Correct Pattern:** Escape exactly ONCE:
```xml
<mxCell id="2" value="&lt;b&gt;Title&lt;/b&gt;" ... />
```

---

## AP-007: Using true/false for Boolean Style Properties

**Pattern:** Using JavaScript/JSON-style boolean strings in Draw.io style strings.

```
<!-- NEVER DO THIS -->
rounded=true;dashed=true;shadow=true;html=true;
```

**Why It Fails:** Draw.io style properties use `1`/`0` exclusively. The string `"true"` is not recognized and the property is ignored.

**Correct Pattern:**
```
rounded=1;dashed=1;shadow=1;html=1;
```

---

## AP-008: Omitting html=1 from Styles

**Pattern:** Creating cells without the `html=1` style property.

```
<!-- NEVER DO THIS -->
rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;fillColor=#DAE8FC;
```

**Why It Fails:** Without `html=1`, labels render as plain text. Line breaks, bold, italic, and all HTML formatting are ignored.

**Correct Pattern:** ALWAYS include `html=1`:
```
rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#DAE8FC;
```

---

## AP-009: Wrong Perimeter for Shape Type

**Pattern:** Using the default rectangle perimeter on non-rectangular shapes.

```
<!-- NEVER DO THIS -->
ellipse;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;
rhombus;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;
triangle;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;
```

**Why It Fails:** Without an explicit perimeter, shapes other than rectangles use `rectanglePerimeter` by default. Edges connect to the bounding box instead of the actual shape boundary.

**Correct Pattern:** ALWAYS set the matching perimeter:
```
ellipse;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;perimeter=ellipsePerimeter;
rhombus;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;perimeter=rhombusPerimeter;
triangle;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;perimeter=trianglePerimeter;
```

---

## AP-010: Edge Geometry Without relative="1"

**Pattern:** Creating edge geometry without the relative flag.

```xml
<!-- NEVER DO THIS -->
<mxCell id="e1" edge="1" source="2" target="3" style="endArrow=classic;html=1;">
  <mxGeometry as="geometry" />
</mxCell>
```

**Why It Fails:** Edge label position and waypoint calculations require relative coordinates. Without `relative="1"`, labels appear at the canvas origin.

**Correct Pattern:**
```xml
<mxCell id="e1" edge="1" source="2" target="3" style="endArrow=classic;html=1;">
  <mxGeometry relative="1" as="geometry" />
</mxCell>
```

---

## AP-011: Using CSS Property Names in Style Strings

**Pattern:** Using CSS property names instead of Draw.io style property names.

```
<!-- NEVER DO THIS -->
background-color=#DAE8FC;border-color=#6C8EBF;font-size=14px;text-align=center;border-width=2;
```

**Why It Fails:** Draw.io uses its own property names (camelCase), not CSS property names (kebab-case). Unknown properties are silently ignored.

**Correct Pattern:**
```
fillColor=#DAE8FC;strokeColor=#6C8EBF;fontSize=14;align=center;strokeWidth=2;
```

---

## AP-012: Missing whiteSpace=wrap on Labeled Shapes

**Pattern:** Creating shapes with text labels but no wrap property.

```
<!-- NEVER DO THIS -->
rounded=1;html=1;fillColor=#DAE8FC;
```

**Why It Fails:** Long labels extend beyond shape boundaries as a single unbroken line, making the diagram unreadable.

**Correct Pattern:**
```
rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#DAE8FC;
```

---

## AP-013: Groups Without container=1

**Pattern:** Creating parent shapes without the container flag.

```
<!-- NEVER DO THIS -->
style="swimlane;html=1;startSize=30;"
```

**Why It Fails:** Without `container=1`, child shapes do not track the parent when moved. Coordinates behave as absolute instead of relative.

**Correct Pattern:**
```
style="swimlane;html=1;startSize=30;container=1;collapsible=0;"
```

---

## AP-014: Referencing Nonexistent Source/Target on Edges

**Pattern:** Edge source or target IDs that do not match any vertex in the diagram.

```xml
<!-- NEVER DO THIS -->
<mxCell id="e1" edge="1" source="nodeA" target="nodeB"
        style="endArrow=classic;html=1;" parent="1">
  <mxGeometry relative="1" as="geometry" />
</mxCell>
<!-- No cells with id="nodeA" or id="nodeB" exist -->
```

**Why It Fails:** The edge renders as a floating line at the canvas origin, or does not render at all.

**Correct Pattern:** ALWAYS create vertex cells BEFORE edges that reference them:
```xml
<mxCell id="2" value="Node A" vertex="1" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;">
  <mxGeometry x="100" y="100" width="120" height="60" as="geometry" />
</mxCell>
<mxCell id="3" value="Node B" vertex="1" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;">
  <mxGeometry x="350" y="100" width="120" height="60" as="geometry" />
</mxCell>
<mxCell id="4" edge="1" source="2" target="3" style="endArrow=classic;html=1;" parent="1">
  <mxGeometry relative="1" as="geometry" />
</mxCell>
```

---

## AP-015: Spaces in Style String Syntax

**Pattern:** Adding spaces around `=` signs or between properties without semicolons.

```
<!-- NEVER DO THIS -->
rounded = 1; whiteSpace = wrap; html = 1;
rounded=1 whiteSpace=wrap html=1
```

**Why It Fails:** The style parser splits on `;` and `=` without trimming whitespace. Spaces become part of property names or values, causing lookup failures.

**Correct Pattern:** NO spaces around `=`, semicolons between all properties:
```
rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;
```

---

## Summary: The 5 Most Dangerous Anti-Patterns

| Rank | Anti-Pattern | Frequency | Impact |
|------|-------------|-----------|--------|
| 1 | AP-004: Inventing shape names | Very High | Shapes render as plain rectangles |
| 2 | AP-002: Omitting structural cells | High | Diagram completely fails to load |
| 3 | AP-001: Generating compressed content | High | Diagram shows blank page |
| 4 | AP-003: Reusing cell IDs | Medium | Cells silently disappear |
| 5 | AP-005: Raw HTML in value | Medium | XML parse error, file unreadable |
